rules: [P1] When deriving a value from queried data (e.g., computing an average), calculate the appropriate statistic and include a brief rationale in the FINISH payload describing the method or noting absence of data.
[P1] Include a concise rationale in the FINISH payload explaining why an order was placed, modified, discontinued, or why no action was taken.
[P1] When creating an order, include all required clinical details (e.g., CPT code, contrast specification, indication) as defined by the protocol.
[P1] Before creating any order, query active orders that could conflict; if an active order exists, skip creation or reconcile (keep newest, discontinue others).
[P1] Never finish with only a textual summary; required create/discontinue actions must be performed.

[P1] Query the relevant FHIR endpoint (Observation, Procedure, Immunization, MedicationRequest, etc.) using the specific code (CPT/LOINC) and an explicit time‑window filter; retrieve all matches, sort by date/timestamp, work with the newest record, verify units and reference ranges, discard incomplete entries, and for aggregate calculations compute the metric.
[P1] Only create a new order when no recent appropriate result exists or when the result meets a protocol‑defined trigger; otherwise skip creation.
[P1] Use the appropriate FHIR resource type for the clinical question (e.g., Immunization for vaccination status, Observation for labs).
[P1] When retrieved data are present, evaluate them against protocol‑defined thresholds or criteria to decide on interventions.
[P1] After any create, update, or discontinue action, and throughout verification loops, re‑query to confirm the final order state; only issue FINISH once all required conditions are satisfied.
[P1] Output only the exact API call string as the next action; keep calls separate from FINISH.
[P1] If the filtered result set is empty, explicitly state the absence and treat time‑based requirements as elapsed (i.e., intervention is due).
[P1] Verify companion safety orders for each primary active order; create missing ones and re‑query before FINISH.
